prompt_text: Open source or affordable HR tools instead of HiBob
response:
If you want **open-source** or **low-cost** alternatives to HiBob, the strongest options in the results are **Frappe HR**, **OrangeHRM Starter**, **Horilla**, **MintHCM**, and **Open HRMS**.[1][2][3][4][8]

- **Frappe HR** — a **100% open-source** HR and payroll system with modules for employee management, onboarding, leave, attendance, payroll, taxation, performance, and recruitment.[1][6]
- **OrangeHRM Starter** — a **free and open-source** HR software aimed at small teams and growing businesses, with cloud or on-premise deployment options.[3]
- **Horilla** — positioned as a **free, open-source** HRMS with attendance, leave, onboarding, recruitment, payroll, and asset management; it also offers self-hosting.[2][7]
- **MintHCM** — a **free and open-source** HCM system where the main cost is your own server upkeep.[4]
- **Open HRMS** — an open-source HR management platform for small to enterprise use, covering core HR processes and extending into onboarding, attendance, benefits, performance, and recruitment.[8]

If you want the closest fit by use case:

| Need | Best fit | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Full HR + payroll, open source | **Frappe HR** | Broad module set and explicitly 100% open source.[1][6] |
| Free starter HRIS with flexibility | **OrangeHRM Starter** | Free/open-source and available cloud or on-premise.[3] |
| Self-hosted, no-license-fee option | **Horilla** | Emphasizes free, open-source, and host-yourself deployment.[2] |
| Enterprise-style HCM | **MintHCM** | Open-source HCM focused on employee journey/processes.[4] |
| Broader HR suite with multiple business functions | **Open HRMS** | Covers many HR workflows across company sizes.[8] |

A practical caveat: open-source HR tools often reduce licensing costs, but implementation, hosting, maintenance, and optional support can still add cost.[5][4]

If you want, I can also narrow these down by **company size**, **must-have features** such as payroll or performance reviews, or whether you want **self-hosted vs cloud**.
